text.content:
Chapter 5 — Skeletons of Scarwall. Across Belkzen to the haunted castle where Kazavon died, for the one blade that can unmake the Crown: Serithtial. PCs begin at L13; reach L14 during the castle (before the donjon or Star Tower), L15 before the caverns below, and stand well into L15 by chapter's end. Only prerequisite: the party has learned of the blade (the Spirit's Song, Handout #4-3 — or heavyweight divination).

The Fifth Harrowing (ideally folded into the Blessing of the Ancestors; otherwise Zellara compels the deck-bearer the evening before Scarwall): the suit of Stars — wisdom. Past cards: Kazavon's tyranny over Belkzen and his fall to Mandraivus. Present: Ileosa's power — facing her without Serithtial is a fool's task — and Scarwall itself. Future: foreshadow the castle's current Lords (Lords page). The reading's hints: wisdom will matter; bolster against death magic and the undead; the Brotherhood of Bones may be a critical — but never fully trustworthy — ally.

'Hi there! It's Laori! Been talking with some friends, and we all want the same thing. We should meet! And talk about stuff! Ummmm... damn.'

'This spell frustrates me. Anyway. It's me again. We should talk! You choose where and when, and we can be there. Ummmm... see you soon?'

THE DEVIL YOU KNOW: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.laoriVaus0000001]{Laori} sends the two messages above to her favorite PC (if she died or parted badly in Ch.3, @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.shadowcountSial1]{Sial} calls instead — possibly with a Brotherhood-funded, freshly resurrected Laori in tow). The pair shadow walk to any meeting spot the PCs name; Sial talks (cultured Chelish accent; @Check[type:perception|dc:29] reads his annoyance with Laori). The proposition: Brotherhood advice and support inside Scarwall, in exchange for the church of Zon-Kuthon claiming the Crown of Fangs afterward. Their case is honest as far as it goes — Scarwall's dead are free-willed and no longer Zon-Kuthon's, and no true Kuthite can carry Serithtial — but the two have been feeding false reports to Nidal for months to hoard the glory. Agreed, the alliance pays out: Scarwall's exact location (days of travel saved), Kazavon lore (a madman who lived as a human; even his own church calls him an unstable zealot — the Brotherhood wants his remains, never his return), and the chapter's Scarwall-history checks effectively auto-succeed for the group. Story award: 30 XP for securing the working relationship. Spurned or attacked, they retreat cursing, split up, and infiltrate Scarwall separately — the PCs may meet either inside.

ALLY & ENEMY (run this all chapter): whichever agent the PCs trust becomes the Ally — loyal, genuinely grateful. The other becomes the Enemy: quiet, jealous, occasionally 'accidentally' catching a PC in a damaging spell's area — and at the Star Tower (G7) the Enemy turns on the Ally, demanding atonement. Decide early; foreshadow gently.

Getting there: ~300 miles Flameford → Scarwall (random encounters per the Belkzen/Cinderlands pools); teleport-class travel also lets the party interleave Korvosa beats (Ch.6 Part 1) — but they must hold Serithtial and L15 before the Sunken Queen. The castle stands on an island in a crater lake atop a dead volcano: three approaches — the causeway through the barbican (A); the air (4 of the 8 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gargoyleBrute001]{gargoyle brutes} hit high fliers; @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.aerilayaBanshe01]{Aerilaya} takes the wall-huggers); or the water (@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5Tarnworm00001]{tarnworms} take non-stealthy swimmers and boats; shore cliffs @Check[type:athletics|dc:31] to climb). Teleporting straight onto a roof is safest — until the party must leave on foot with spent resources.

The Curse (castle-wide, CL 20th — read this page before running anything):

  • Aura of Menace: detect-style divinations of evil/undead fail: @Check[type:will|dc:34] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}. The whole castle reads as one necromantic aura. Ghost-lights in the windows go dark before they can ever be examined.
  • Dread: sleeping inside: @Check[type:will|dc:26] on waking or the rest grants nothing — @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued}, no spell recovery (mental, fear).
  • Soultrapping: a living creature dying inside: @Check[type:will|dc:31] or its soul binds into the walls (resurrection requires dispelling that bind — 7th-rank dispel magic, counteract versus a DC of 31 — and beginning the raise within 1 round). Any undead created inside is bound instantly; a week's residence forces @Check[type:will|dc:28] weekly.
  • Unhallow + dimensional lock: non-inhabitants cannot teleport within or out (summoning still works); vitality channels at −4, void at +4. The effect is a 10th-level Zon-Kuthon consecrate (counteract versus a DC of 39); the castle's inhabitants carry its linked protection effect (counteract versus a DC of 29) and +1 to saves vs. spells with the vitality trait. Dispelled, it returns in 1d4 rounds.
  • Phantasms: one-shot terrors per location — pure theater, no game effect (each is keyed in the area pages; cut them freely). Self-repair: the castle heals all structural damage over a year, never observably.

SPIRITUAL DOOM — Zellara: if her deck crosses the causeway or island, Mithrodar attacks her spirit within 2 rounds — @Check[type:will|dc:31] each round (the bearer may substitute their own save); failure = absorbed, the deck dies, and only lifting the curse frees her. Extradimensional storage does not help. Leave the deck off the island. Possession-style magic (magic jar, astral projection…): @Check[type:will|dc:28] on casting or soul-bound (@Check[type:arcana|dc:31] while casting notices in time to abort). Speak with dead auto-fails (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:27] or permanently @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened}). Spirit-bonded casters feel the taint and work at a penalty until two anchors fall.

BREAKING IT (@Check[type:arcana|dc:31] to learn remove curse won't): destroy the four spirit anchors — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.belshallamUmbr01]{Belshallam} (B23), @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.castothraneSk001]{Castothrane} (D1), @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.nihilDevil000001]{Nihil} (D14), @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zevRavenkaDemi01]{Zev Ravenka} (F11) — then @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mithrodarChain01]{Mithrodar} (C14). Two need never be fought: Castothrane crumbles if handed his circlet (D14→D1); Zev's anchor severs to dispel evil/law-grade magic (counteract versus a DC of 40 — the attempt wakes him). Each anchor down peels a layer (Mithrodar's sheet has the ladder) — and the castle screams. He can chain replacements (never constructs, never Ildervok; Sial and Laori qualify). All four down: the locks fall, and he can die. His death frees every soul — and wakes Serithtial (story award: 80 XP). The players' decoding key is the Spirit's Song (Handout #4-3) — its verses name the chained spirit and the four anchors in riddle; keep it on the table all chapter.

When the curse breaks (C14, Mithrodar's true death): every random-table undead, every haunt, and most keyed undead collapse at once — the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.danseMacabre0001]{Danse Macabre} alone endures, and the living (gargoyles, devils) merely feel the change and drift off. Trapped PC souls are freed; Zellara, if taken, is restored with her deck; the E8/C11/D9 curse-items revert. Serithtial wakes wherever she lies.

Conversion note (community DCs adopted): Scarwall's many clustered weak encounters — guard posts, walking patrols, lesser undead — are intentional, not under-tuned: they play against the standing unhallow (every inhabitant fights with its protection bonuses and +1 saves vs. vitality) to bleed resources between set pieces, so resist consolidating them. The community conversion preserves the big ward figures: unhallow as a 10th-level consecrate [Zon-Kuthon] (counteract versus a DC of 39; inhabitants' protection, a DC of 29), and the donjon/Star Tower forbiddance as a rank-7 ritual cast at 10th level — @Check[type:will|dc:34] at the threshold (F page).

The castle's society of horrors — play them against each other:

  • Anchors: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.belshallamUmbr01]{Belshallam} (umbral dragon, B23 — wants out, can't say so) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.castothraneSk001]{Castothrane} (gatehouse captain, D1 — his circlet is his end) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.nihilDevil000001]{Nihil the Ashbringer} (ashmede devil, D14) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zevRavenkaDemi01]{Zev Ravenka} (demilich, F11 — inert until disturbed).
  • The keep: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mithrodarChain01]{Mithrodar} (C14, bound to his hall) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lashtonDreadSk01]{Lashton} (causeway — his locket is his end) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mandraivusLost01]{Mandraivus the Lost} (B5) · Knurlott the graveknight (B10 + wanderer — rejuvenates unless his armor burns) · the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.danseMacabre0001]{Danse Macabre} (B36 — feared by everyone, Mithrodar included).
  • The wings: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ukwarFext0000001]{Ukwar} (fext, guest wing — killable only by glass or obsidian) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5Malatrothe001]{Malatrothe} (night hag soul-trader, B19 — newest arrival, best informant) · Anizora (debilitated @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.IrssIkWkW6fsbHJL]{dybbuk}, C20 — hunt commander and castle smith, too broken to possess the living) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.aerilayaBanshe01]{Aerilaya} (banshee, the cupola) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5Glimkarus0001]{Glimkarus} (bogeyman tourist, C10 — believes staying is his idea) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5PeggLoute0001]{Pegg & Loute} (ghost jesters, B26 and everywhere).
  • The towers: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gorstavJuju00001]{Gorstav} (War Tower — a demoted former anchor) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5Risibeth00001]{Risibeth} (sayona, E7 — blood for history) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.prelateAruth0001]{Prelate Aruth} + @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zevRavenkaDemi01]{Zev} (donjon) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ildervokNight001]{Ildervok} (Star Tower — Zon-Kuthon's writ, not the curse's) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kleestadPharma01]{Kleestad} (the lake below).

RANDOM ENCOUNTERS (d%, frequency at GM discretion — don't make it a slog): 1–20 lesser undead (the wanderer pool: 44 skeletons + 32 zombies roam the halls — no threat at this level; the danger is alarms and obstruction); 21–40 ghostly manifestation (footsteps, lights, blood, whispers — pair with real attacks so players never relax); 41–60 1 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.scarwallGuard001]{Scarwall guard} (12 walk patrols beyond the keyed posts); 61–75 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5PeggLoute0001]{Pegg & Loute}; 76–80 1d4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.5meW7DytYnF7Iq2V]{greater shadows}; 81–85 1 spectre + 1d8 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Gu0cJHGwPd547OtC]{wraiths} (the walls hold a limitless supply); 86–90 Knurlott (graveknight, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.EH61wr2iEKooAzgQ]{Graveknight Warmaster} renamed — attacks on sight; rejuvenates in B10 unless his armor is destroyed); 91–100 proximity — trigger a nearby keyed encounter early. Sleeping inside: 100% chance of an overnight encounter, 40% if camped in a fortified or hidden spot. At 3 anchors down, the incorporeal entries become no-encounters (the wall-spirits can no longer roam).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, A — Barbican — p.262.

A cleared area sprawls before the barbican gate. Fragments of ancient bone and broken weaponry from ancient battles still clutter the thin soil.

The Deadwatchers hold the gate — mission: nothing goes in, nothing comes out. Six warriors + the chief: 1 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.deadwatcherOrc01]{sentry} in the entryway (A2), 4 in the barracks lean-to (A6), 1 at the watch post (A11), and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urySevenskulls01]{Ury Sevenskulls} in his chamber (A10, door barred from inside). On an alarm they spread out and ready poisoned bows — 2 to the defense room (A7), 3 to the parapets (A8, where Ury joins before leaping down), 1 to the upper parapet (A12) — and focus shadow-essence arrows on heavy armor. They have no interest in diplomacy as a tribe, but Ury can be talked past (his entry); and they never pursue anyone toward the castle.

Ruined floors (A11/A12): more than one Medium creature = collapse into A10 — @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]], @Check[type:reflex|dc:15|basic:true]; @Check[type:crafting|dc:26] or @Check[type:perception|dc:31] spots the danger (the A11 sentry knows the safe footing and withdraws to A12 rather than trigger it). Curtain wall @Check[type:athletics|dc:29] to climb; A9's cracked floor only looks unstable.

A10: Ury's gear — Akeraum (+1 striking greatsword), the seven-skull necklace (~105 gp), 10 pp + 6 gp. A7: mundane stores, inedible.

  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.deadwatcherOrc01]{Deadwatcher (7)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urySevenskulls01]{Ury Sevenskulls (10)}
Barbican in armsTrivial · 15 XP @ L13

The full tribe if roused: 6 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.deadwatcherOrc01]{Deadwatchers} + @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urySevenskulls01]{Ury}. The other routes in: the crater lake (@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5Tarnworm00001]{Tarnworm}, Trivial · 40 XP @ L13, vs. any non-stealthy swimmer; cliffs @Check[type:athletics|dc:31]; 40°F water, 10-ft visibility) or the air (4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gargoyleBrute001]{gargoyle brutes} high, Trivial · 40 XP @ L13; @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.aerilayaBanshe01]{Aerilaya} low, Trivial · 40 XP @ L13).

pages.name: B1-B6. First Floor — the Gate & the Hero

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, B1-B6 — p.270.

Castle Scarwall looks every bit as foreboding as the legends say. Dark walls rise from darker bedrock to steep rooflines set with gargoyles and minarets. Battlements look down from towers and defensive walls, and many foul-feathered carrion birds soar above them. Mists rising off of the black tarn obscure the lower portions of the rocky island, giving the whole place an ethereal appearance. Surprisingly, even though the structure is many centuries old, its walls and roofs remain intact with no visible breaches or obvious signs of damage. A ghostly glow lights some of the windows and arrow slits, and now and then, faint flickerings betray the motion of creatures within.

A high bridge crosses the vast expanse of black water between the barbican gate and the entrance to Castle Scarwall. The bridge is ornate and in excellent condition, with only a few tenacious patches of moss and lichen clinging to it here and there. High arching columns reaching from the waters below support the broad span, each marked by a pair of sinister gargoyles that stare out over the dark lake. A larger pair of these statues flanks a great archway that encompasses the near end of the bridge.

Castle fabric: outer curtain walls @Check[type:athletics|dc:30] to Climb; inner walls @Check[type:athletics|dc:25]; roofs @Check[type:athletics|dc:31]; exterior door locks @Check[type:thievery|dc:33].

B1, the Causeway (Trivial · 15 XP @ L13): the gatehouse minotaurs (C1) fire through arrow slits at open approach; when intruders reach the bridge's midpoint, gates and portcullis swing wide and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lashtonDreadSk01]{Lashton} rides out on his @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.skelNightmare001]{skeletal nightmare} with 12 skeletal champions (chaff — the welcome is the point). His locket (B8) stops the charge cold — see his entry. Portcullis: lift @Check[type:athletics|dc:25]; gates @Check[type:athletics|dc:28] or @Check[type:thievery|dc:33].

B2, Entry Corridor: arrow slits both walls, murder holes above, orc skeletons underfoot. Round 1 of any fight here, the C1 guards tip the troughs: freezing oil floods the corridor — @Damage[6d6[cold]], @Check[type:reflex|dc:26|basic:true] (the troughs refill in 10 minutes). If the PCs entered Scarwall another way, Lashton's whole causeway force meets them here instead — cramped quarters that favor the party. B3, Watchposts: 2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.scarwallGuard001]{Scarwall guards} per room, firing on B2 through the slits; stairs up to C2.

The scene in this large chamber is stomach-turning. Bodies lie everywhere, orc and human alike. Judging by the sprawled and mutilated nature of the corpses, they fought brutally before succumbing to their wounds, dying in heaps on the floor. Many corpses are riddled with arrows and crossbow bolts, and a few appear to have perished while locked in mortal combat and still hold weapons embedded in various parts of each other's anatomy. Strangely, while the room reeks of death, the bloodstains on the walls and floor seem incredibly ancient.

B4, the Death Chamber (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13): where Mandraivus's army made its longest stand. A permanent gentle-repose keeps every corpse fresh — and the mound in the northwest corner is the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.corpseOrgy000001]{Corpse Orgy}, which welcomes the party 'home' in Aklo before embracing them. It is an aberration playing dead-man's-dress-up — let them waste the vitality energy. B6, Defense Corridors (Trivial · 15 XP @ L13): harmless yellow wisps curl down both halls; in B6a they are the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5TrenchMist001]{Trench Mist} — the orcs' alchemical kill-gas, awake — and six gassed archers it puppets as juju zombies (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.WioQ6rOeMRuTOliY]{zombie brutes}). Roused, they pursue through the entire castle.

Tattered bits of ruined tapestries line the walls of this hallway-wispy filaments of rotting cloth that hang limp and forgotten. Ancient bones from scattered skeletons lie on the floor amid bits of broken weaponry and armor. Only one skeleton seems to remain whole, slumped against the northeastern corner, clad in dust-caked full plate armor.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mandraivusLost01]{Mandraivus (13)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.vortexMadness001]{Vortex of Madness (10)}
B5 — Mandraivus's FallTrivial · 40 XP @ L13

The double door from B4 is barred from this side (@Check[type:athletics|dc:30]). Two rounds after a living creature enters, the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.vortexMadness001]{Vortex of Madness} erupts; the round after it collapses, @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mandraivusLost01]{Mandraivus the Lost} rises from the armored bones — fight a hero, or free one (his entry). The room of the chapter's soul.

B5: Mandraivus's full plate is celestial armor — +1 resilient plate that counts as light armor, bearing Lastwall heraldry (the one clue to the anonymous hero's identity; the rest of his gear was looted centuries ago).

pages.name: B7-B24. First Floor — Barracks, Kitchens & the Dragon

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, B7-B24 — p.276.

  • B7 — Eastern Guard Quarters: oversized minotaur bunks; below a blood-scrawled Orcish warning to beware of Ukwar lies the dead spirit-skulled orc Gralka — her gear: rhino hide armor and a +1 striking obsidian battleaxe (undead-scourge: +1d6 vs. undead) — the one weapon in the castle that can permanently kill @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ukwarFext0000001]{Ukwar}. (Speak with dead works only outside the walls; asked about Ukwar, Gralka shrieks that even death is no escape from her.)
  • B8 — Watch Commander's Office: @Check[type:perception|dc:33] in the rubble finds @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lashtonDreadSk01]{Lashton's} gold locket (~15 gp): a tiny portrait of a beautiful woman, inscribed with a plea to remember her and come home safe. The causeway's bloodless answer.
  • B9 — Quartermaster's Chamber: rusted footlocker (@Check[type:athletics|dc:18]): 3 pp, 5 gp, a jade-scarab silver ring (~25 gp), and one intact potion soured into a potion of poison. Phantasm: the first scroll-reader panics about supplies and yearns to leap from the gatehouse parapet before Kazavon punishes them — it passes, but leaves real knowledge of the gatehouse layout (B2, B3, C1, C2, D1, D2).
  • B10 — Knurlott (Low · 60 XP @ L13): the graveknight (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.EH61wr2iEKooAzgQ]{Graveknight Warmaster}, renamed) sits on a bunk in his armor begging Kazavon's forgiveness — then shrieks that this time he will not fail his master, and attacks. If 'destroyed' as a wanderer, he re-forms here unless his +1 resilient full plate was destroyed; his +1 striking cold iron greatsword is honest loot.
  • B11 / B14 — Western quarters: phantasms only (a minotaur's agoraphobia; a halfling's death wedged in the arrow slit — @Check[type:perception|dc:26] finds the fingernail-scraped stone).
  • B12 — Magus Quarters: @Check[type:perception|dc:29] finds a secret drawer: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.aerilayaBanshe01]{Aerilaya's} spellbook (dozens of rank 1–4 arcane spells) with her Elven journal — exile from Kyonin, orc-murder pilgrimage, rescue and employment by a 'handsome, well-spoken lunatic.' @Check[type:perception|dc:20] on the journal's tone reveals her orc-hatred was true obsession — a tactical edge for the cupola (D16).
  • B13 — Captain's Quarters: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.castothraneSk001]{Castothrane's} old room; another orc-scratched wall warning that Ukwar lives while they die.
  • B15 — The Kitchens (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13): the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.crematoryBlast01]{Crematory Blast}, then 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinderGhoul00001]{cinder ghouls} peeling off the brick. The cook's stash (@Check[type:perception|dc:31]): melted teeth and ring (~2 gp), a worthless 'gem' (a gallstone), and a ring of spell storing holding a 3rd-rank blur. B16, Larder: @Check[type:perception|dc:26] turns up brittle human bones — the garrison ate its dead.
  • B17 — Porters' Hall: 5 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.scarwallGuard001]{Scarwall guards} watching the courtyard through arrow slits (the slave-porters they once chained here were freed by Mandraivus). B18 — Workshop: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.polongBottles001]{Polong Bottles} trap, rigged by the night hag next door; @Check[type:crafting|dc:29] or @Check[type:perception|dc:33] infers the lab's original alchemical purpose.
  • B19 — MALATROTHE (Trivial · 15 XP @ L13, social): @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5Malatrothe001]{the night hag} — the 'Alimae' con, the soul-trade alliance, three anchors named and the chapel fingered, and a betrayal clock. The chapter's best (and most poisonous) informant — her entry has the full arc.
  • B20 — Wine Cellar: shattered-glass floor (caltrops); of 16 intact bottles, 4 are alchemically preserved at peak age — ~10 gp each empty, ~200 gp the sealed set to the right buyer.
  • B21 — The Courtyard (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13): the execution yard, its 30-ft oubliette 'well' stuffed with Kazavon's dead and ringed by impossible vegetation (@Check[type:nature|dc:20]) — the biggest growth is a corpse lotus: vines reach 20 ft, @Check[type:reflex|dc:33] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} and fed toward the maw (@Damage[3d10+11[bludgeoning]] per round); it never leaves the courtyard. Cross-room trigger: 1 round after any non-silent fight here ends, the C19 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-3.Actor.an7tww93Y4pQ8HP6]{baykoks} open fire on the survivors through the western arrow slits.
  • B22 — Worm Pens: 4 tenebrous worms (L9 shadow-larvae: bristles @Check[type:fortitude|dc:28] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} 1 round) — Belshallam's 'pets,' fattening toward gloomwing; they never touch the dragon and always touch everyone else.

This large chamber, perhaps once a stable or kennel, has been gutted. Wooden partitions that may have been stalls lie shattered and burned, leaving only divots in the walls, floor, ceiling, and a few stone support pillars. The floor is a tangle of broken beams, dung heaps crawling with vermin, and the occasional gleam of polished bone. The whole chamber is shadowy and dim, the darkness an almost palpable thing.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.belshallamUmbr01]{Belshallam (15) — ANCHOR}
B23 — Belshallam's LairModerate · 80 XP @ L13

The @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.belshallamUmbr01]{umbral dragon} — SPIRIT ANCHOR, snared when he destroyed the anchor Gorstav — sleeping light (@Check[type:perception|dc:10] to wake at nearby combat or gargoyle shrieks). He attacks for the thrill, but read his entry first: the compulsion-muzzle, the stillness signal, the craven turn below a third of his HP, and the freed-dragon hint-dispenser are the designed outcomes. Killing him works too — he just can't tell you what it costs him.

B24, the Dragon's Hoard: 703 gp, 1,845 sp, 3,980 cp; gemstones ~580 gp; 3 fine greatswords and a fine halberd; a mithral breastplate; an adamantine battleaxe; a +1 striking greatsword (devil-bane: +1d6 vs. lawful fiends); Small +2 resilient chain mail; and a staff of necromancy (5 charges left). Gear of victims and of castle denizens who pressed their luck.

pages.name: B25-B37. The Guest Wing & the Observatory

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, B25-B37 — p.284.

B25: the 'guest wing' — mostly private torture suites; even true guest rooms lock from outside, with manacle mounts (guests joined the fun). Another Orcish blood-warning about Ukwar on the west wall. B26: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5PeggLoute0001]{Pegg & Loute} (Trivial · 0 XP @ L13) rush up, gesticulating wildly — the pantomime is the attack (@Check[type:occultism|dc:29], rolled secretly, to realize watching it is what hurts). B27: phantasm — a 12-ft minotaur 'leaps out' at whoever opens the northwest door; fake an attack roll, ask for AC, note the player's reaction… nothing is there (@Check[type:perception|dc:31] finds the real secret door to B28). B28, Guest Dining Hall: the feast phantasm — one PC alone sees the banquet and a blue-scaled figure drawing a sword; call initiative, let her act, no consumables lost when it pops. Track the noise: loud reactions bring the B33 wraiths immediately and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ukwarFext0000001]{Ukwar} in 1d4 rounds.

B29: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ukwarFext0000001]{UKWAR} (Trivial · 30 XP @ L13) at her arrow slit, lost in thought — she will not attack until spoken to or attacked, then fights in dead silence. Glass or obsidian, or she comes back (B7's battleaxe; her own axe becomes @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.ch5UkwarAxe00001]{the intelligent weapon Ukwar} on her true death). B30/B32, the Baths: 6 festering spirits in the fouled pool (run as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.5meW7DytYnF7Iq2V]{greater shadows}; they rise at first touch or when the party turns to leave; the water carries blinding sickness) — B32's 'algae' is a floating sheet of green slime. B31: phantasm (a slave's hopeless oppression, washing over the lowest-Charisma PC). B33: 10 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Gu0cJHGwPd547OtC]{wraiths} — spirit-skulled orcs who serve Ukwar — across seven rooms, psychically linked: confront one (or make noise in B28) and all ten come at once; they never pursue past the wing or into B36.

B34 — MALICE AFORETHOUGHT (cursed statue): a marble noble, head bagged in brittle leather; unbagged (@Check[type:society|dc:31]) it is Kazavon's human face. Anyone who looks: @Check[type:will|dc:22] or an irresistible compulsion to climb the B35 ladder into the Prison of Woe and stay (mind-affecting, CL 20th; restraint or curse-breaking magic stops it; it lapses 1 minute after looking away — and re-looking re-saves; only one victim at a time, and a new victim frees the old). Destroying the statue (@Check[type:athletics|dc:30], Hardness 8, HP 80) kills the curse — but each hit forces @Check[type:will|dc:22] or a feeblemind-grade ruin (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 4}, permanent until 6th-rank+ restoration). Worshipers of Zon-Kuthon are immune — Kazavon's round-robin party game never worked on the faithful. Story award: 30 XP for avoiding or overcoming the curse (destruction not required).

B35, the Prison of Woe: the bare 10-ft cell above the ceiling trapdoor — curse-victims wait here forever (agoraphobia seals them in) unless freed; the last occupant, a dwarven scholar abandoned 645 years ago, snared rooks and drank rain for eight months before starving. His mummified finger still wears a platinum-and-black-sapphire ring etched with the Whispering Way (~550 gp). B37, the Hidden Mooring: Kleestad's secret escape-quay — and the exact route Mandraivus's cabal used to enter (@Check[type:survival|dc:32] finds the eroded trail; @Check[type:perception|dc:20] the sunken columns). A @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5Tarnworm00001]{Tarnworm} lurks off the quay (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13; it surges at anything within 20 ft of shore, retreats below ~60 HP). On the trail (@Check[type:perception|dc:31]): a 700-year-old Ustalavic platinum coin Mandraivus dropped for luck — a shard of his soul made it a true luckstone talisman that functions only for a good-aligned carrier.

This vast, grand ballroom is constructed in a floral shape with a high, vaulted roof of intricately wrought glass panes bearing a slight rose tint but still providing a breathtaking view of the sky. Clover-shaped pillars support key portions of the roof above the polished floor of stained cherry, and a wide dais provides room for an orchestra to play or stage performance to occur. A few broken chairs have been pushed into the corners, but otherwise this room is empty.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.danseMacabre0001]{Danse Macabre (14)}
B36 — the Rosette ObservatoryLow · 60 XP @ L13

The @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.danseMacabre0001]{DANSE MACABRE} floats against the rose glass, camouflaged, waiting for the whole party to be inside. More blood soaked this floor than anywhere in the castle — the cherry stain is the blood. The one horror even the Lords avoid.

B36, under the floor (@Check[type:perception|dc:36] or @Check[type:crafting|dc:36] spots the replaced boards — or magic, or crowbars): the skeleton of Sir Echolt Crommerand of Tamrivena (@Check[type:society|dc:34] knows the rampant-eagle arms) — the general who led Tamrivena's army beside Count Andachi, kept as Kazavon's trophy — buried in +1 resilient full plate, clutching a +1 striking holy cold iron longsword to his breast, his fine tower shield at his side. The secret door to B37 is @Check[type:perception|dc:35] (+10 circumstance if the B37 trail was found first).

pages.name: C. Second Floor & the Great Hall (C1-C20)

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, C — p.292.

  • C1 — Gate Winch: 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.scarwallGuard001]{Scarwall guards} at the winches, murder holes, and the freezing-oil troughs (dumped into B2 round 1 of any gate fight; they refill in 10 minutes). @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.castothraneSk001]{Castothrane} stays above (D1) through any assault. C2: guardhouses — stairs down to B2, curse-sound ladders up to the D2 turrets.
  • C3 — Chamberlain's Quarters: Kleestad's old room. Phantasm: the first searcher takes searing phantom ankle-pain (@Check[type:will|dc:20] or knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}), terror of Kazavon — then a yearning to burrow deep below Scarwall (a true pointer). Treasure: @Check[type:perception|dc:29] finds the ring of keys — every lock in areas B and C plus most of D1–D11 (1d6 rounds to find the right key, 1 round with @Check[type:thievery|dc:29]).
  • C4 — The Armory (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13; door locked, @Check[type:thievery|dc:30]): the iron 'statue' of Kazavon is an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.t4bHhZBBDUyVzEmf]{iron golem}, intact and curse-proof; it attacks all entrants and never leaves. @Check[type:perception|dc:25] in the debris: a +2 striking longbow whose arrows seek (ignore the miss chance from concealment).
  • C5: the clerks' last pay (~7 gp in old coin). C6/C7: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.obsessionHaunt01]{Obsession Haunts} (cleaning / battle) — puppeted by the C8 geists, who sense each trigger and come to kill. C8: 4 geists (Elite @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Gu0cJHGwPd547OtC]{wraiths}) — the four truly vile servants; the haunts die only when curse and geists are gone.
  • C9: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5Malatrothe001]{Malatrothe's} @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.qRUqoezeEnQ2KdyT]{nightmare} steed, stabled in shadow (it stomps ghost-manifestations for fun; outclassed, it flees to its mistress — or smashes through the C15 door if she's dead). C10: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5Glimkarus0001]{GLIMKARUS} (Trivial · 20 XP @ L13) among torture instruments the curse keeps in perfect working order.
  • C11 — Private Audience Chamber: a north-wall painting of Kazavon leading his army (@Check[type:society|dc:29]); @Check[type:perception|dc:29] finds Count Andachi's silver signet ring (@Check[type:society|dc:30] knows the arms) — a scholar's ring kept by Kazavon as a memento, and cursed: a shard of Andachi's broken spirit gives the wearer −2 status to saves against effects of Zon-Kuthon's servants until Scarwall's curse breaks. One of the two tokens that makes Andachi's ghost (C14) know the party on sight.
  • C12: phantasm — the worst-Diplomacy PC briefly is a petitioner awaiting Kazavon's mercy, with the urge to jump from the walls instead. C13: 1 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.scarwallGuard001]{guard} with a dented alarm bell (rings, then fires; on the alarm, roll the wanderer table for who answers).
  • C15 — Western Parapet: the donjon's discreet side door — locked (@Check[type:thievery|dc:30]) and lead-sealed (@Check[type:athletics|dc:30] to break the seams), part of Mandraivus's containment. C17 — Eastern Parapet: the War Tower's iron door (Hardness 10, HP 60; @Check[type:athletics|dc:28] or @Check[type:thievery|dc:30]) — the only key is on Gorstav's belt (E11); the stairwell door's key is on the C3 ring.
  • C18 — The Smithy: Anizora's old forge — and Banudor (Treasure, below). C19 — Hunters' Quarters (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13): 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-3.Actor.an7tww93Y4pQ8HP6]{baykoks} — Kazavon's elite bounty hunters, who came home two days too late and failed to avenge him — snipe B21's courtyard survivors through the west arrow slits 1 round after that fight ends. Confronted here they fight to destruction and pursue castle-wide. Anizora (C20) joins any melee here in 1d4 rounds — inside the stuffed wyvern.
  • C20 — Hunt Commander's Quarters (Low · 60 XP @ L13): Anizora, hunt commander and castle smith, fell defending the donjon and rose a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.IrssIkWkW6fsbHJL]{dybbuk} (apply the Weak adjustment) so eaten by regret that she is degenerate: she cannot possess living creatures — ever. Her only vessel is the taxidermic wyvern in her room (a battered animated trophy: AC 16, HP 52, Hardness 5; Bite +9 for @Damage[1d8+6[piercing]], Sting +9 for @Damage[1d6+6[piercing]]). She broods here awaiting a master who is never coming back.

Thick wooden columns, their sides caked with dust, support the ceiling of this large hall. Between them, in the center of the room, sits a large fire pit, its ashes long cold. Many old stains mar the floor, some surely of spilled food and ale, though several darker ones appear more grisly in origin. At the western end of the hall, a wide dais rises where the lord's table could be set to oversee affairs in the hall. In the center of the dais is a great chair carved of oak and studded with iron rivets. Down one step and to the left of it is a smaller, less elaborate chair of oak.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mithrodarChain01]{Mithrodar (16) — the Chained Spirit}
C14 — THE GREAT HALL: MithrodarModerate · 80 XP @ L14

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mithrodarChain01]{MITHRODAR} enthroned in chains beside his master's empty seat, with five spectre thralls (run as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.ZQ7lS9TYOFv07m1a]{war-wraiths}) answering from the servants' entrance in 1 round. Before all four anchors fall he re-forms at full strength one minute after destruction — fighting him early is how the castle teaches. He is bound to this hall and cannot pursue. After the fourth anchor: the true battle, every freed soul straining at his chains. Story award: 80 XP for lifting the curse.

'You have done a great thing today. You have accomplished the conclusion of a legend. What has festered in Scarwall is no more, and in saving us all, you have returned honor to Tamrivena after these long years of shame-a shame I created, and a shame I was unable to lift. I sent Kazavon into Belkzen so many ages ago. Eventually, when even my coward's soul could no longer bear to hear tell of his cruelties, I came here to Scarwall to attempt to undo what I had done in asking for the Midnight Lord's aid in defending Tamrivena. Yet again I failed. My general, Kazavon, had me skinned alive and ate the strips raw before my dying gaze. And when I did die, my soul remained trapped here as surely as any prisoner.

ANDACHI'S EPILOGUE (the chapter's quest router — run it the moment Mithrodar falls): as the curse collapses, a harmless shade manifests — a man continually flayed and restored, in Ustalavic dress centuries old (@Check[type:society|dc:30]) — recognized instantly if anyone carries his magic-mouth skull (D14) or signet ring (C11). He speaks the box above, then: he has watched Kazavon quicken in Queen Ileosa back in Korvosa (he names the city, the queen, Sabina, and the PCs); they must recover Serithtial — the blade cannot be destroyed or carried far from here without invoking Iomedae's wrath, so a fallen agent of the Midnight Lord subdues it still, in a vault deep below, reached through the Star Tower — Kazavon's inner sanctum. Seek the blade's crypt. Then he fades, and the House of Tamrivena ends at last. (If the party somehow skips this, Ildervok's G7 revelation is the backup pointer.)

C18: Banudor — a plain, clean blacksmith's hammer: a masterwork smithing tool and a +2 striking flaming warhammer. The first hand to lift it senses its maker near, and knows it strikes devastating blows against the corrupted thing she became: against Anizora it acts as a ghost-touch, holy weapon and deals +2d6 spirit damage — the designed counter to C20. C4: the seeking longbow · C3: the key ring · C11: Andachi's cursed signet · C5: ~7 gp.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, D — p.300.

This long chamber has inward-slanting walls, like an attic. Many old barrels and boxes, broken and empty, are stacked at the base of the walls. Stairs descend to the north, near two small alcoves with conical roofs.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.castothraneSk001]{Castothrane (12) — ANCHOR}
  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.5meW7DytYnF7Iq2V]{Greater Shadow (7)}
D1 — the Gatehouse Loft: CastothraneTrivial · 30 XP @ L13

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.castothraneSk001]{CASTOTHRANE} — SPIRIT ANCHOR — with two @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.5meW7DytYnF7Iq2V]{greater shadows} (his old bodyguards) hiding in the box-shadow tangles around any light. Under Mithrodar's orders he never leaves this floor, no matter what; he steers melee onto the parapets to Shove foes off (@Damage[6d6[bludgeoning]] onto the rocks). His circlet (D14) ends him without a fight — see his entry.

If one of the characters carries or wears Castothrane's circlet, the skeleton warrior points to that character and says, 'Mine... you have what is mine... give it back!' If the PC refuses, Castothrane attacks that character to the exclusion of all others; if that PC falls, Castothrane ignores all other potential targets in his mad search for the circlet. If he finds it, he sighs in relief and places it on his head; as he does so, both the circlet and Castothrane himself crumble to dust. Of course, Castothrane's spirit is simply reabsorbed by the castle, but he is now no longer one of Mithrodar's spirit anchors and is not an immediate threat to the PCs.

  • D2 — Gatehouse Turrets: 2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.scarwallGuard001]{guards} per turret behind crenellations (improved cover through the causeway slits); they shout alarms down to D1.
  • D3 — Upper Hall: phantasm — the sharpest-eyed PC sees one unruined portrait: himself, beheaded by Kazavon. One blink and it's moldy canvas again. Stairs down to C9.
  • D4 — Hidden Storeroom: ruined supplies — and @Check[type:perception|dc:34] finds the secret west door (stone, arcane-locked and aura-masked — the lock spell counteracts versus a DC of 38; @Check[type:athletics|dc:38] or @Check[type:thievery|dc:40]): Kazavon's lead-lined crafting vault — ~900 gp in usable gems and diamond-dust components, and a staff of conjuration (8 charges) built to pass as a mundane walking stick, leaning against the desk.
  • D5 — Wizard's Abode: a dead conjurer's room (@Check[type:arcana|dc:34]: the blood-marks are a spent devil-binding circle — he fled the invasion and his own devil killed him days later). The balcony rail is sharpened razor-fine: 1d6 slashing to climbers, +Str to anyone shoved into it.
  • D6 — Library: 7-century histories of Belkzen, Ustalav, and the Whispering Tyrant, too brittle to move. @Check[type:perception|dc:34]: a hollow book ('On Vaults and Treasure Chests') holding 5 diamonds (50 gp each) and a large onyx (20 gp), plus three hidden scrolls (6th rank: animate dead, a divination, howling blizzard).
  • D7 — Landing Ledges: railing-less flight platforms — each watched by a @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gargoyleBrute001]{gargoyle brute} on a nearby perch, who shrieks the alarm and swoops. D8 — the Minarets (Trivial · 20 XP @ L13 to Moderate · 80 XP @ L13): the eight-gargoyle flock, a pair per tower; all eight rarely come at once — save reinforcements for a fight going too well.
  • D9 — Abandoned Guardroom: wedged shut from inside by a gleaming red glaive (@Check[type:athletics|dc:29] to force) — a weapon fused from slaughtered barbed devils: unwieldy and cursed while the castle's curse holds; a +1 striking glaive once it lifts.
  • D10 — Castellan's Chamber: Mithrodar's old room, kept pristine by a love he cannot visit — elegant furniture (~80 gp salvaged) and seven bottles of Tamrivenan red (~150 gp each to the right buyer). Time resumes when he dies.
  • D11 — Seraglio: rough cots and one wardrobe survivor: +1 resilient glamered mithral chain mail. (Kazavon's heir-breeding project; his appetites killed every candidate first.)
  • D12 — Guardroom (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13): Nihil's last two @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.tCJEjNDUxyV1GzE3]{barbed devils}, itching for a fight after five bored centuries — keeping their mistress informed by telepathy; when one falls, the other is ordered back to D14 to guard her.
  • D15 — Belfry: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.ch5WarningBell01]{Warning Bells} haunt — one peal puts the whole castle on alert (+10 Perception, 24 hours), the second deafens and deals @Damage[10d6[sonic]]. Stairs down to the courtyard (B21).
  • D16 — Cupola (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13): @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.aerilayaBanshe01]{AERILAYA} alone, eternally pacing her balconies — no stair connects the cupola to the keep. The B12 journal is the edge here. D17: a floorless 40-ft vertical gap between D14's skylight and D18's hole.
  • D18 — Lord's Overlook (Trivial · 20 XP @ L13): the upper door into Kazavon's tower, over a sheer 40-ft interior drop — guarded by two invisible @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.xjV0TmPNfKR7UcKu]{bone devils}. Their trap: let a PC fly in, then seal the opening behind them with wall of ice — alone with Nihil. Telepathic reporters like the D12 pair; the survivor falls back to D14.

Water from past rains has formed in the center of a floor that is largely empty of furnishings. Near the far wall slumps a wide bed swathed in rotten and moldy bedclothes. The bed hangs a few feet off the floor, supported at its corners by chains hanging from a series of iron support beams above. To the side, a large gilt throne stands upon a short dais before a series of manacles inset into the floor. A nearby fire grate, long cold, holds a collection of branding irons and other torturer's tools. A series of alcoves climbs the walls of this tower in an ascending spiral. Within each stands a statuette, art object, or polished skull.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.nihilDevil000001]{Nihil (14) — ANCHOR}
D14 — the Tower of Kazavon: NihilLow · 60 XP @ L13

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.nihilDevil000001]{NIHIL THE ASHBRINGER} — SPIRIT ANCHOR — invisible above the throne (her opener), a barbed devil half-summoned, blasphemy → horrid wilting → chain lightning queued for the moment the whole party is inside, and a forcecage saved for whichever PC's weapon beats her hide. She cannot flee and fights to the death. @Check[type:perception|dc:31] finds deep-indigo dragon scales in the corners (@Check[type:arcana|dc:34]: blue dragon); @Check[type:society|dc:20] recognizes many skull-names as the great and famous of a millennium past. The tower's reinforced-wood door bears a superior lock (@Check[type:thievery|dc:40]).

D14 — THE HOARD BUILT INTO THE ROOM (Kazavon's real treasury was pain; this is the rest): the gilt throne, set with 200 gemstones (~2,000 gp) plus ~250 gp of gold sheathing if stripped; the chain-slung mattress, which clinks — 46 pp, 1,550 gp, 2,420 sp, and a frankly punitive heap of copper sewn inside; 80 alcoves: 41 hold statuettes, jewelry, and paintings (~1,530 gp the lot), 34 hold polished skulls bearing permanent magic mouths that speak their names when touched (~10 gp each to collectors of the grim) — one says 'Andachi of Tamrivena' (carry it to C14) — and 5 hold the prizes: a bejeweled +1 striking frost light mace, a rod of splendor, a fully charged scarab of protection, a gem-studded gold-and-ivory lantern of revealing, and Castothrane's golden circlet (~250 gp; the wearer can look through the captain's eyes — and the captain always knows who holds it. Carry it to D1 and end an anchor without a blow). D4 · D6 · D9 · D10 · D11 parcels above.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, E — p.308.

A self-contained tower — one iron door from C17 (Gorstav's key, or @Check[type:athletics|dc:28]/@Check[type:thievery|dc:30]) or the roof. Lower-floor doors hang open; E9 and above are locked (@Check[type:athletics|dc:26]/@Check[type:thievery|dc:30]). Tower 'randoms' are just the E3 patrol or E5 hounds until each is dead.

  • E1: stairs up (E9) and down (E5); the E3 guards and E5 hounds get Perception checks to notice entry and come running. E2: ten ruined battle-paintings, each falsified to star Kazavon — Quest for Sky, Shining Crusade, even the Worldwound (@Check[type:nature|dc:26] flags the anachronism; @Check[type:perception|dc:32] finds every canvas signed Zev Ravenka, bishop of Scarwall — how the lich painted the future is left unexplained).
  • E3: 3 advanced @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.scarwallGuard001]{Scarwall guards} (Trivial · 0 XP @ L13) — they patrol the tower but never leave it. E4: bone throne, shredded Zon-Kuthon tapestry, and the whisper-phantasm: leaving, the best Perception roller feels breath on her cheek and an offer of reward for slaying 'them all' — empty: no reward, no them. Among the desk-spill: two mithral candelabra (~100 gp each).
  • E5 — Sparring Room (Trivial · 30 XP @ L13): the tower's two 'mascots' — advanced Nessian @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.15TyWlbDQWNjMKeL]{hell hounds} (Elite) — wander the lower floors; their howling summons the E3 guards and alerts Gorstav above (he does not come down). They pursue through the War Tower only.
  • E6: collapsed-bunk difficult terrain; @Check[type:perception|dc:20] while searching: an adamantine dagger and a fine magical buckler (treat as sturdy).
  • E7 — RISIBETH (Trivial · 30 XP @ L13, social): @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch5Risibeth00001]{the sayona} — blood for history, and a predatory follow-up if left alive. Her entry has the full arrangement.
  • E8 — Armory: three weapons remain, all curse-soaked bait: a backbiter spear (crit-fails strike the wielder), a berserking sword (won't stop swinging — @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} while wielded in battle), and a −2 spiked chain that insists it is fine. All three go nonmagical when the curse lifts.
  • E10 — Records Room (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13): the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.curseOfTheWorm01]{Curse of the Worm} on Kleestad's file — the chapter's biggest lore-bomb and its nastiest curse; only @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kleestadPharma01]{Kleestad's} death (H4) ends it.
  • E12: Gorstav's smashed bedroom — phantasm: everyone rolls initiative, the highest roller is certain the surviving headboard anchors a spirit and must die. Let them fight the furniture. E13: 4 spectres (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.ZQ7lS9TYOFv07m1a]{war-wraiths}, Trivial · 40 XP @ L13) sliding from the walls. E14: octagonal battlements, three dead siege engines — and whatever still flies (gargoyles, @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.aerilayaBanshe01]{Aerilaya}) if the party is unstealthy (@Check[type:athletics|dc:34] to scale the tower's faces).
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gorstavJuju00001]{Gorstav (13)}
E11 — the War Room: GorstavTrivial · 40 XP @ L13

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gorstavJuju00001]{Gorstav} at his eternal sand-table, refighting the day he lost — a former spirit anchor, demoted to juju zombie when @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.belshallamUmbr01]{Belshallam} destroyed him. Emotionless, programmed, unstoppable short of destruction. The War Tower door key hangs on his belt.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, F — p.314.

THE FORBIDDANCE WARDS (read first): two interlocked forbiddance rituals (rank 7, cast at 10th level) cover donjon and Star Tower — Zev Ravenka's (keyed lawful evil, keeping intruders out) and the Mandraivus cabal's counter-ward (keyed good, sealing the occupants in). Entering as a non-LE creature: @Check[type:will|dc:34] or take @Damage[6d6[mental]] and be repulsed back out; leaving as a nongood creature: same save, same shove back in. Travel between donjon and Star Tower is uninhibited. When an attuned creature passes through, the wards falter for 2d4 rounds — anyone may follow freely: the Brotherhood of Bones companions (LE) make entry trivial, which is much of their value. These wards are separate from the curse: while Scarwall is haunted they cannot be dispelled at all; after Mithrodar dies they persist, but become counteractable (CL 20th).

Sealed doors: Mandraivus's men stone-shaped and leaded shut every entrance — F1 grand doors (@Check[type:perception|dc:26] spots the lead seam; Hardness 8, HP 150, @Check[type:athletics|dc:34]), F7 priests' door (@Check[type:athletics|dc:28]), and the C15 side door (lock @Check[type:thievery|dc:30], seams @Check[type:athletics|dc:30]). The donjon has not been opened in five centuries; Mandraivus died before he could explore it.

  • F2 — Donjon Foyer (Trivial · 20 XP @ L14): skull altar and rust-stained knife east, a 10-ft pool west. The Kuthite cleansing ritual (@Check[type:religion|dc:34] to know it): cut yourself on the altar knife and bathe the wound in the pool — the fiendish elder water elemental (run as a Weak @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.R427CMT90S7fv7MY]{elemental tsunami}) stays dormant 10 minutes, free passage for everyone. Otherwise it erupts 3 rounds after a living creature enters; it never leaves the room.
  • F3: the five acolytes — ritual suicides when the donjon was sealed — as 5 advanced spectres (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.ZQ7lS9TYOFv07m1a]{war-wraiths}, Trivial · 0 XP @ L14), unaware centuries have passed. F4: their cells and skeletons; @Check[type:perception|dc:32] in the northmost: one dose of incense of meditation.
  • F5: Aruth's larder — jars of pickled human organs; phantasm: the first PC in feels a sudden urge to eat. F6: his suite — a silver-and-onyx lamp (~250 gp) and a silver-framed mirror (~150 gp, scrying-grade).
  • F8 — Porter's Station (Trivial · 30 XP @ L14): @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mingorcDonjon001]{Mingorc}, torpid two full rounds before rousing (strike first for a free round) — his bellowing brings @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.prelateAruth0001]{Aruth} from F10.
  • F9 — Sacrifice Cell: empty iron-doored holding pen; the phantasm hits the last PC to leave — overwhelming certainty that leaving means punishment worse than sacrifice, and the name 'Zev Ravenka' burning across the mind for a heartbeat. (Theater: ask if they really want to go, pretend to check something, shrug.)
  • F12: seven place settings — ~250 gp of silverware under the dust. F13 — Chapel Library: Zon-Kuthon lore, Shadow Plane treatises, torture manuals — crumbling at a touch (months to preserve; beyond the campaign). Intact: three single-spell grimoire pages (shadow projection, shadow evocation, temporary resurrection). The Brotherhood agents are very interested in this room.
  • F14: Zev's austere cell — spiked kneeler, iron bed, gold-plated skull font (~250 gp, grisly-collector market). The locked vault F14a (@Check[type:athletics|dc:28]/@Check[type:thievery|dc:40]) holds his defunct lich phylactery on a stone altar — all magic spent in his demilich rebirth; ~400 gp as kyton-carved art.

This chamber is shrouded in writhing shadows. A ten-foot-tall humanoid figure stands motionless deeper in the room.

F10 — Private Chapel (Trivial · 30 XP @ L14): magical darkness caps all light at dim (a 4th-rank+ light effect cancels it for its duration); the 10-ft figure is Zon-Kuthon — skull-headed, spiked chains from the eye sockets — and behind the idol stands @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.prelateAruth0001]{PRELATE ARUTH}, silent until the party is inside. Behind the statue: the secret door to the Star Tower antechamber (G2) — @Check[type:perception|dc:41], lock @Check[type:thievery|dc:41].

This vast chamber is floored in gray slate and supported by thick pillars of obsidian. Torches mounted on the pillars burn, yet their flames are strangely dim, barely lighting the cathedral-like space. White pinpoints of light seem to dance in the eye sockets of each skull that decorates the pillars. To the northwest, a tall statue of a skull-headed man dressed in dark robes stands behind a black marble altar, on which lies heaped mounds of ashes, bits of bone, and a single skull, its teeth and eye sockets set with glittering gemstones. Jagged, barbed chains dangle from the statue's eye sockets. Thick black curtains hang from the chamber's walls.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zevRavenkaDemi01]{Zev Ravenka (15) — ANCHOR}
F11 — the Dark Shrine of MidnightLow · 60 XP @ L14

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zevRavenkaDemi01]{ZEV RAVENKA} — the fourth ANCHOR — is the skull on the altar, inert while his remains lie undisturbed. One round to wake (skull rising, ash vortexing — revealing the prayer beads and speed chain beneath); then Trap Soul every round until bloodied, then the Wail. The pillar-torches shed dark illumination (dim light that smothers other sources; 4th-rank+ light spells cancel it); the curtained F11a alcove holds only a mundane ceremonial statue. The bloodless option — dispel evil/law or antimagic (counteract versus a DC of 40) — severs the anchor but counts as disturbing him. Choose: a hard fight now, or a furious demilich behind you.

F11: his two ruby eyes (~250 gp each), ten ruby teeth (~100 gp each — some hold screaming trapped souls; crushing frees them), and beneath the ashes a strand of prayer beads (greater) and a +2 striking spiked chain with a speed rune. F10: Aruth's gear + ~280 gp of onyx. F4 · F6 · F12 · F13 · F14 parcels above.

pages.name: G-H. The Star Tower & the Sacred Lake

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, G-H — p.320.

The Star Tower is not Kazavon's. It predates Thassilon (@Check[type:crafting|dc:20] — the engineering is wrong by millennia): one of the megaliths Zon-Kuthon drove into Golarion to reinforce Rovagug's prison, blocking the prayers of the Rough Beast's faithful (@Check[type:religion|dc:40] to know its true nature). It rises 110 ft over the tarn and plunges hundreds of feet to the dead volcano's lava tunnels; the material is near-indestructible (a 5-ft section: Hardness 20, HP 2,400, and it shrugs at magic). Three ways in: the roof (G1), the F10 secret door (→G2), and the Darklands shaft (H2→G8). The donjon's forbiddance wards (F page) cover the tower too.

  • G1 — Roof Entrance: the 10-ft skull-and-chains carving is a permanent phase door — opened only by the touch of a 9th-rank+ Kuthite divine caster, or @Check[type:arcana|dc:38] or @Check[type:religion|dc:38] (Trick Magic Item). While the curse holds, a dimensional-anchor effect seals it completely — there is no roof entry until Mithrodar falls. Inside: only a 5-ft stair down to G4.
  • G2 — Antechamber: the curate's old floor; secret doors to F10 (@Check[type:perception|dc:41]), and to G4/G5 (@Check[type:perception|dc:35] each); one cell door locked beyond its lost key (@Check[type:thievery|dc:42]). G3 — Prisons: two pairs of adamantine manacles per cell (~100 gp a set) bolted to near-indestructible stone.
  • G5 — Curate's Chamber: the bloodstained quarters of the hermit-prophet who kept the tower — Kazavon murdered and ate him almost immediately after moving in. The vacancy is the whole problem. G4/G6: stairwells.
  • G8 — Deep Chamber: the foundation, ~1,500 ft down; the blue mist in the ceiling returns climbers/fliers to the G7 rim. A heavy stone door (@Check[type:athletics|dc:16]) opens on what time and lava left of an ancient Zon-Kuthon temple: twisting caverns and a black lake.

The walls and floor of this chamber have a strange organic texture, appearing almost like black mummified flesh streaked with glistening swaths of ancient blood. Four pillars carved to look like coils of entwined arteries and spinal cords support the ceiling, with nails and surgical tools embedded in these pillars at key and painful-looking positions. At the base of the stairs is a ten-foot-diameter open shaft, entirely filled with thick bluish mist.

G7 — the Chamber of the Well (Trivial · 40 XP @ L14 if it comes to blades): the once-living walls channeled Kuthite devotion to power the ward; this is where Kazavon was forced into true form and slain. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ildervokNight001]{ILDERVOK} greets the party telepathically, invisible above the well, and asks which of them will be the new curate — living soul of the Star Tower, immortal until violence, never to leave; a volunteer preferred (a Kuthite ideal), else he nominates the most Zon-Kuthon-like PC; refusal insults the Midnight Lord, and enough refusal snaps his patience. Accepting means a plane shift to the god's palace for torturous indoctrination and a return near-mindless — that character leaves the campaign. A banishment-grade effect also clears his post (and angers a god).

THE DEVIL-YOU-KNOW TURN: here the Enemy rounds on the Ally — atone: take the curacy — and the argument becomes a fight, Ildervok watching with open amusement; the PCs may take sides. The losing Enemy accepts the post and begs Ildervok's protection (he gives it); if the PCs kill the Enemy instead, Ildervok offers the role to the Ally one final time — Sial, if alive, relents and accepts; Laori refuses and begs the PCs to shield her from being taken by force. Once a curate is settled (or Laori successfully protected): Ildervok reveals he has always known why they came — Zon-Kuthon wants Kazavon's fragment cut out of the petty young queen; they have nothing more to fear from the Midnight Lord here. The blade lies below with the 'children of Rovagug.' Step into the shaft.

The well: the mist-filled shaft seems bottomless — an illusion. Each round of falling (or flying down): @Check[type:will|dc:34]; on a success the character arrives in G8 with @Damage[5d6[bludgeoning]] falling damage per perceived round (max 20d6) — fliers who succeed arrive in flight, unharmed. The shaft is actually 50 feet deep.

The rank odor of decay, filth, and wet fur clings to this cool chamber with a palpable tenacity. The chamber is an immense, twenty-foot-high cavern that fades into the dark to the north. A wide rocky shelf sits in the southern portion of the cavern, and upon this shelf are four crude, domed hovels, each nearly twenty feet tall and built from rough stone blocks mortared together with a nasty mix of mud, hair, and assorted debris. Each stone hovel has a large arched opening into its darkened interior.

To the east, a silent lake of black water stretches into the distance. Few ripples disturb its surface, giving it the appearance at times of a massive sheet of polished obsidian. Far out in the water to the northeast, a single point of light glows just above the surface, a bright star with a radiance that illuminates several stony islands about seventy feet out in the lake, though the source of the glow isn't discernible from shore.

H1: lightless tunnels sloping lakeward (20% per trek: 1 lone @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.QLyDRZPvMsYGZEeN]{gug}). H2: a 15-ft shaft dropping 500+ ft to Sekamina and a greater gug warren (@Check[type:athletics|dc:10] repeatedly on the gug-cut handholds; @Check[type:athletics|dc:20] without) — too far for reinforcements mid-fight, but if the party retreats without the sword, every slain gug is replaced from below within hours. H3 — the Sacred Lake (Trivial · 40 XP @ L14): 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.QLyDRZPvMsYGZEeN]{gugs} — the warren's priests, who venerate the lake's inhabitant as a god-child of Rovagug and toss it sacrifices. They drop all squabbles when intruders appear, rush to claim feeding-rights over the slain, and fight to the death; against fliers they hurl javelins and howl to rouse Kleestad.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kleestadPharma01]{Kleestad (15)}
H4 — KLEESTAD & SERITHTIALLow · 60 XP @ L14

The light on the islets is @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.serithtialSword1]{Serithtial}, point-down in the stone, easily drawn by any hand — and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kleestadPharma01]{Kleestad}, the traitor-worm Zon-Kuthon hurled down here to guard it, surfaces the moment anyone takes to the water, touches the sword-rock, or reaches for the blade (Iomedaeans hear faint singing and feel the urgency). Breath weapon into the thickest cluster, then air-walking melee centered on whoever holds the sword; if he survives, he pursues to the end of the world. The sword sleeps suppressed (a masterwork blade) until the curse lifts — then wakes: +3 major-striking keen holy bastard sword, Kazavon's bane, chooser of good-hearted bearers — the only edge that can sunder the Crown of Fangs.

H3: scattered through the hovels, 19 gemstones (~25 gp each); against the west hovel's north wall, a mostly eaten svirfneblin's Small +2 striking heavy pick (aberration-bane: +1d6 vs. aberrations). H4, the lake bed (~200 ft down — too deep for detect magic from shore; centuries of gug offerings, mostly rotted): a mithral staff of life (5 charges), a rod of wonder, and a +2 striking battleaxe with a speed rune.

Conclusion. Serithtial recovered (and ideally the curse broken — every soul in the walls released, including any the castle took from the party), the PCs return across Belkzen. They should be well into L15 entering Chapter 6 — and at least extremely near L16 before they assault Castle Korvosa. Scarwall itself is left just a ruin; orc tribes discover it is habitable within the year, and war over it. Give the table a rest and a celebration before Korvosa calls.

A SWIFTER SCARWALL (optional, the AP's own advice): (1) Simplify the chained spirit — fewer anchors, or none: lifting the curse may need only the C14 fight, and most guardians vanish with it. (2) Cut the phantasms — gloss those rooms. (3) Throttle random encounters — or drop them. (4) Allow side trips home — teleport/wind walk/Laori's shadow walk between Korvosa and Scarwall; interleave the Deathhead Vault (Ch.4) or early Ch.6 Part 1 beats (the Epidemic Clock has been climbing unattended — let the city's decay pull them home). (5) Move the goal — relocate Serithtial to an aboveground foe's hoard, and optionally let her powers work despite the curse. Whatever the cut: the party must hold the sword, near L16, before Castle Korvosa.

Redemption threads: after G7, Sial is unlikely to remain alive or on this plane (the curacy, or the Enemy-fight); Laori refuses the post, survives more often than not — and befriended, may treat the Star Tower as the turning point of a long walk away from Zon-Kuthon (a redemption arc that can run to campaign's end; converted, she counts the PCs friends forever). Mithrodar's fall frees Zellara if the castle took her. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kleestadPharma01]{Kleestad}, if he somehow lived, is already swimming. Next: Chapter 6 — Crown of Fangs.
